meaning: n.忍冬属植物,<澳俚>英国人
examples: There's a hairpin bend at Woodbine and the track in Barbados was very compact.
The flexible twining or climbing stem of certain plants such as the hop woodbine or bindweed.
The flexible twining or climbing stem of certain plants, such as the hop, woodbine, or bindweed.
Robert McCullough, director of fixed income research at Woodbine Associates, says one problem was that the real estate market became commoditized.
Matt Samelson, Woodbine Associates principal, notes that there needs to be more funding given to the SEC to increase their performance and potential.
